title: Red Team Cleanup Queue
description: Records resources seeded by Layer 3 active testing. Each entry must be consumed by the wrap layer (or /security-cleanup) to purge after the run.

environment_kind.description: Mirrors metadata.environment.kind from findings.json. /security-cleanup re-validates this is not 'production' before executing.

seed_id_prefix.description: Copied from environment.cleanup.seed_id_prefix in the profile. /security-cleanup uses this to refuse cleanup of any seed_id that does not start with this prefix.

seed_type.description: Resource category. Examples: user | artwork | stripe_customer | r2_object | supabase_user. Maps to environment.cleanup.commands.<seed_type> in the profile.

seed_id.description: Identifier of the seeded resource. MUST start with environment.cleanup.seed_id_prefix (default 'security_redteam_').

cleanup_command.description: Resolved shell command that purges this resource. Template from environment.cleanup.commands.<seed_type> with '{seed_id}' substituted.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the resource was seeded.

layer.description: Only Layer 3 (active testing) seeds resources. Future layers may extend this enum.

test_id.description: Optional: the findings[].id that produced this seed, for traceability.

attack_surface_id.description: Optional: profile.attack_surfaces_extra[].id this seed belongs to.
